title: 3D-RFT: Reinforcement Fine-Tuning for Video-based 3D Scene Understanding
abstract: The paper introduces 3D-RFT, the first framework to extend RLVR (Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards) to video-based 3D perception and reasoning. Existing methods rely on Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), where token-level cross-entropy loss acts as an indirect proxy for optimization, causing misalignment between training goals and performance metrics. 3D-RFT directly optimizes the model towards evaluation metrics using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) with task-specific reward functions based on metrics like 3D IoU and F1-Score. The approach first activates 3D-aware Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) via SFT, then applies reinforcement fine-tuning. Experiments show 3D-RFT-4B achieves state-of-the-art performance on various video-based 3D scene understanding tasks and significantly outperforms larger models (e.g., VGLLM-8B) on benchmarks for 3D video detection, 3D visual grounding, and spatial reasoning.
note: arXiv preprint submitted 5 March 2026. User reports accepted at ICML 2026. Categories: cs.CV, cs.AI.

opensource.summary: Official code on GitHub and model checkpoints on HuggingFace are available.
